text: This fibrous tumor is being peeled from the left carotid artery when there's an avulsion of a small perforator.
This avulsion creates two tiny holes within the carotid artery.
The bleeding is controlled with a suction while bipolar cautery is used to weld the perforator closed again.
You can see the dynamic endoscopy allows the operating surgeon to work with two hands controlling the bleeding and introducing the bipolar cautery into the field.
This also provides a very up-close view of the bleeding at the time, which allows this type of microscopic surgical technique to be applied.
By coagulating closed, the small openings in the artery, the vessel is preserved.
As seen here, it's just covered with Surgicel and fat.
There's some data that shows muscle patch directly onto the artery may have a better chance of preventing pseudoaneurysm.
Fibrin glue was applied, followed by a fat graft.
